reasoning: Paid loss triangle demonstrates longest tail among dollar measures—expected materially longer development than incurred. Starting age 143 selected over 203 to retain maximum observations (n_factors_in_fit=12, cv=0.0182). Although is_monotone_from_here=False (slope_sign_changes=6), this reflects typical paid development volatility rather than structural breaks; low coefficient of variation (0.0182 < 0.15) confirms underlying stability. Exponential methods (exp_dev_quick, exp_dev_product, exp_dev_exact_last) rejected despite stable LOO diagnostics (loo_std_dev=0.00180) due to poor fit quality (R²=0.1899, well below 0.85 threshold) and lack of clear curve specification. Bondy method selected because materiality anchor applies: tail factor of 1.0039 represents only 5.77% of CDF, clearly immaterial to reserve estimate. Gap rule satisfied (gap_flag=False for all scenarios). Bondy provides conservative, stable baseline when tail is immaterial. McClenahan (r=0.4545) and mcclenahan at age 203 rejected as they force tail to 1.0 despite stronger closure signal in paid data. Selection balances materiality principle with development data constraints.
